abstract: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the emerging technologies poised to define fifth-generation (5G) cellular wireless networks. Driven by the demand for higher data rates, improved quality-of-experience, and ultra-low latency, the 5G ecosystem is shifting toward a multi-tier architecture characterized by network densification and advanced spectral efficiency. The authors examine several key enabling pillars, including massive MIMO and millimeter-wave communications, full-duplex transmission, and dense heterogeneous networks. Furthermore, the paper explores transformative architectural shifts such as Cloud-Radio Access Networks (C-RAN) and wireless network virtualization, alongside sustainable solutions like RF energy harvesting. Beyond highlighting these technologies, the article identifies critical research challenges in radio resource management and discusses the complex measurement and testing requirements necessary to validate 5G components. This review serves as a roadmap for understanding the integration of diverse technologies into a unified, high-performance mobile standard.
